item.content:encoded:
Nice! Vogel really had a hell of a life, huh?

I was expecting a "You do not recognized the bodies in the water." after the whole Claire thing, honestly. :P

Really good! It reminds me a lot of Dan Simmons' The Terror, especially with the supernatural polar bear.

However, my only suggestion is that the camera percentage might want to have a more rapid rate of battery usage, since cold can be a big impact on a battery's functionality. Then again, that's more of a personal issue, and not a glaring error.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I quite liked it, although after segment D it got a bit….weird, if that's the right term to use? it felt cold, no pun intended.

+1

item.title: Author post
item.content:encoded:
Titled, "Wherever you go, There you are"

Grief is not a new theme for me to explore in my SCPs. I'm not sure what that says about me, but, writing is an important way for me to work through my emotions and the contents of the articles don't necessarily reflect what I am trying to work with. But I have seen grief take its toll.

This story resonates with the idea that…no matter what you do, or where you turn to for your problems, or all of that other stuff…you yourself are still apart of the equation. Until you address the rot at your core, you can changed a million variables and never change your circumstance.

Don't run from your grief, you'll just die tired.

Thank you for your read.

Credits to Cerastes for the name "Wheat Watchers"
Credits to plaidypus for the preliminary read and formatting help.

Image credits:
"Reflections of a polar bear" by user 'Smudge 9000' is licensed under CC-BY-SA 2.0 at:
https://www.flickr.com/photos/7599112@N08/9545259304
Modified by Lord_of_Laugh
